title: 🎙️ Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling
description: Today we explore how Cariboo and rural BC communities create, preserve, and share culture — from festivals and community radio to digital archives and open-source arts tools. We're looking for stories where creativity meets technology in small-town contexts, and where local artists and storytellers are finding new ways to reach audiences beyond geography. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and rural BC communities create, preserve, and share culture — from festivals and community radio to digital archives and open-source arts tools. We're looking for stories where creativity meets technology in small-town contexts, and where local artists and storytellers are finding new ways to reach audiences beyond geography.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: digital storytelling platforms and tools, community media tech (podcasting, streaming, local publishing), creative tech economy in small towns, rural cultural sector innovation, open-source arts tools, Cariboo arts festivals using digital outreach, preservation of rural cultural heritage through technology, animation/film/media production in rural BC, local radio/podcast infrastructure, and how rural communities use technology for creative expression. Prefer articles that bridge culture AND technology in rural/Cariboo contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [ZDNet] Stopping bugs before they ship: The shift to preventative security
items.content_html: Secure software starts before coding begins. Threat modeling, safer defaults, dependency hygiene, and developer workflow guardrails can help prevent vulnerabilities.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Clueless graduation speaker astonished to find that media students hate AI
items.content_html:
A video from the Spring 2026 Commencement ceremony at the University of Central Florida's Nicholson School of Communication and Media perfectly encapsulates the information divides around AI. Speaker Gloria Caulfield, vice president of strategic alliances at a Bahamas-based private equity fund, blithely hypes AI only to find, to her astonishment, that new media grads do not agree. — Read the rest

The post Clueless graduation speaker astonished to find that media students hate AI appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [New Atlas] Record-breaking timber tower incorporates recycled wind turbine blades
items.content_html:
Rising to a height of 78 m (256 ft), this extraordinary tower sets a new record as Denmark's tallest wooden building – and is one of the tallest in the world too. More than just its scale, though, the project impresses with its use of recycled materials, including everything from wind turbine blades to old windows.

Continue Reading

Category: Architecture, Engineering

Tags: Building and Construction, cross-laminated timber, Timber, Office

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Researchers say AI chatbots may blur the line between reality and delusion
items.content_html: A new study suggests AI chatbots may do more than spread misinformation — they can actively strengthen a user’s false beliefs. Because conversational AI often validates and builds on what users say, it can make distorted memories, conspiracy theories, or delusions feel more believable and emotionally real. Researchers warn that AI companions may be especially risky for isolated or vulnerable people seeking reassurance and connection.

items.title: [Modern Farmer] The Small Farmer’s Guide to Succession Sowing
items.content_html:
Succession sowing is a great way for farmers to maximize their productivity. By continuously sowing seeds throughout the season, you can grow significantly more than by just planting one crop, and that means more goes toward revenue. It’s an easy process when bolstered by the right timing.

The post The Small Farmer’s Guide to Succession Sowing appeared first on Modern Farmer.

items.title: [Open Culture] Nearly 50 Years Later, WKRP in Cincinnati Becomes a Real Radio Station
items.content_html: It took nearly 50 years. WKRP in Cincinnati is no longer just a TV sitcom. It’s now a real radio station in Cincinnati. A Cincy-area FM station, known as “The Oasis,” has adopted the WKRP call letters after acquiring them from a nonprofit radio station in North Carolina. The Raleigh-based station put the call letters […]
